variant.name: variant

secondaryEmphasis.name: secondaryEmphasis
secondaryEmphasis.description:
Tunes the prominence of the secondary value when it renders.
`discrete` (default for stacked) — `xxs` discrete tone, easy to scan past.
`muted` — `xs` secondary tone (legacy behaviour).
`match` — same size as primary, regular weight.
`hidden` — never render the secondary even if a pair is provided.

showOverflow.name: showOverflow
showOverflow.description: Whether to render a `+N` count when `users.length > max`. Default `true`.

overflowFormatter.name: overflowFormatter
overflowFormatter.description: Provide custom overflow text (e.g. "+12 more"). Default `+N`.

avatarClassName.name: avatarClassName
avatarClassName.description: Extra class names applied to each avatar root.

date.name: date
date.description: Date instance, ISO string, or unix timestamp.

time.name: time
time.description: ISO time string (`HH:mm`) or already-formatted time string.

timePlacement.name: timePlacement
timePlacement.description: Where to place the time. Default `inline` for `inline` layout, `below` for `stacked`.

tone.name: tone
tone.description: Tone of the boxed block. Default `default` (card surface).

boxed.name: boxed
boxed.description: Whether to render a card-like box around the block. Default `true` for stacked, `false` for inline.

dayFormat.name: dayFormat
dayFormat.description: Custom format for the day-of-month token (date-fns format). Default `'d'`.

weekdayFormat.name: weekdayFormat
weekdayFormat.description: Custom format for the weekday token. Default `'EEE'`.

monthFormat.name: monthFormat
monthFormat.description: Custom format for the month token. Default `'MMM'`.

variant.name: variant

secondaryEmphasis.name: secondaryEmphasis
secondaryEmphasis.description:
Tunes the prominence of the secondary value when it renders.
`discrete` (default for stacked) — `xxs` discrete tone, easy to scan past.
`muted` — `xs` secondary tone (legacy behaviour).
`match` — same size as primary, regular weight.
`hidden` — never render the secondary even if a pair is provided.

money.name: money
money.description: Optional money payload for migration hints

invalid.name: invalid
invalid.description: Invalid state override

onChangeData.name: onChangeData
onChangeData.description: Optional change handler for combined data

step.name: step
step.description: When set, renders − / + steppers and snaps the value to multiples of `step`.

roundingMode.name: roundingMode
roundingMode.description: How to round when normalising on blur or stepping. Default `'round'`.

normalizeOnBlur.name: normalizeOnBlur
normalizeOnBlur.description: Pad to full decimalPlaces and clamp to [min, max] on blur. Default `true`.

currencies.name: currencies
currencies.description:
Available currencies for the inline picker. Defaults to `['USD', 'EUR']`
— pass your own list or wire `useMoneyConfig` at the consumer site for
real production use.

onCurrencyChange.name: onCurrencyChange
onCurrencyChange.description: Callback when the user changes the currency in the inline picker.

onConfigError.name: onConfigError
onConfigError.description:
Called when the input is misconfigured (mismatched currency/defaultCurrency
while the selector is disabled, missing currency, etc.). The library
also DEV-logs the same message to the console.
onConfigError.type: ((message: string, details: { currency?: string | undefined; defaultCurrency?: string | undefined; disableCurrencySelector?: boolean | undefined; }) => void) | undefined

multiple.name: multiple
multiple.description:
Allow multiple items expanded at once. Defaults to `false` (Radix
single-mode behavior).

defaultValue.name: defaultValue
defaultValue.description: Initial expanded values (uncontrolled). Pass an array of `item.value`.

iconStyle.name: iconStyle
iconStyle.description: How the leading icon is framed. Defaults to `'medallion'`.

strings.name: strings
strings.description: All copy is overridable via the strings prop.

SmartAccordionStrings.description:
Strings for `SmartAccordion`. There aren't many — Radix handles the
trigger ARIA states automatically, so the only library-owned copy is
the optional landmark label on the wrapping nav region. Consumers
override per-item `title` / `badge` / `content` directly via
`SmartAccordionItem` props (the strings live in the data, not here).

SmartAccordionItem.description:
One section in a SmartAccordion. The shape is intentionally narrow —
for richer rows (multi-line metadata, custom layouts), drop down to the
compound-component API (`<Accordion>` + `<AccordionItem>` + …) and
compose freely.

SmartAccordionVariant.description:
Visual variant for the SmartAccordion shell.

`card`     — the canonical admin treatment: each item is a separate
             card with `border-border bg-card rounded-md`, items spaced
             with `space-y-2`. Best for settings pages / FAQ lists.
`bordered` — single rounded shell that wraps every item; rows separated
             by a divider. Tighter visually, good for dense settings
             groups.
`flat`     — no chrome at all (consumer wraps it themselves).

SmartAccordionIconStyle.description:
Whether the icon renders as a circular medallion (the user's
`bg-muted size-8 rounded-full` pattern) or unframed inline.

Badge.description:
Composed Badge using base-ui primitives
- Semantic variants using CSS variables: success | info | warning | error.
- Primary/secondary with proper theming.
- Optional `dot` prop for dot indicator style (colored dot + text, no background).

surface.name: surface
surface.description:
Outer surface chrome. Default `'card'`. Legacy `transparent` still
works (mapped to `'flat'`).

footerSlot.name: footerSlot
footerSlot.description:
Optional full-width footer band rendered under the content, ideal
for a single primary action ("Open", "Connect"). Pairs with
`footerDivider` to add a `border-t` rule above it.

headerDivider.name: headerDivider
headerDivider.description: Render a `border-b` rule between header and content.

footerDivider.name: footerDivider
footerDivider.description: Render a `border-t` rule between content and footer.

expandable.name: expandable
expandable.description:
Make the card body collapsible. Pass `true` for default behavior
(12rem / 192px collapsed max-height with a soft fade overlay) or
an object to tune the collapsed height.
expandable.type: boolean | { collapsedMaxHeight?: string | number | undefined; } | undefined

SmartCardStrings.description:
SmartCard strings — i18n surface for the chrome the consumer doesn't
pass directly. Most copy (title, description, action labels, alert
body) flows in via props; the strings here cover the implicit
affordances (expand toggle).

SmartCardSurface.description:
Outer surface chrome.

- `card` (default): canonical bordered + soft-shadow card surface.
- `flat`: no border, no shadow, transparent background — use when
  nesting inside another surface that already has chrome. Equivalent
  to the legacy `transparent` boolean.
- `framed`: matted "polaroid" treatment — soft 5px outer border tinted
  from `--border` plus a 1px hairline inner border drawn via a
  pseudo-element. All values flow through theme tokens.

closeOnSelect.name: closeOnSelect
closeOnSelect.description:
Whether the dropdown should close after selecting an item.
Default: `true` for single, `false` for multiple.

applyButton.name: applyButton
applyButton.description:
Render an "Apply" / "Cancel" footer instead of committing each
selection immediately. Only meaningful for multi-select. The
working selection lives internally as a draft until `onApply`
commits it to the controlled value.

onApply.name: onApply
onApply.description: Fired when the user clicks Apply (or presses Enter on apply).

onCancel.name: onCancel
onCancel.description: Fired when the user clicks Cancel (closes without committing).

ComboboxRoot.description:
Groups all parts of the combobox.
Doesn't render its own HTML element.

Documentation: [Base UI Combobox](https://base-ui.com/react/components/combobox)

multiple.name: multiple
multiple.description: Whether multiple items can be selected.

autoComplete.name: autoComplete
autoComplete.description:
Provides a hint to the browser for autofill.
@see https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Attributes/autocomplete

autoHighlight.name: autoHighlight
autoHighlight.description: Whether the first matching item is highlighted automatically while filtering.

highlightItemOnHover.name: highlightItemOnHover
highlightItemOnHover.description:
Whether moving the pointer over items should highlight them.
Disabling this prop allows CSS `:hover` to be differentiated from the `:focus` (`data-highlighted`) state.

itemToStringLabel.name: itemToStringLabel
itemToStringLabel.description:
When the item values are objects (`<Combobox.Item value={object}>`), this function converts the object value to a string representation for display in the input.
If the shape of the object is `{ value, label }`, the label will be used automatically without needing to specify this prop.

itemToStringValue.name: itemToStringValue
itemToStringValue.description:
When the item values are objects (`<Combobox.Item value={object}>`), this function converts the object value to a string representation for form submission.
If the shape of the object is `{ value, label }`, the value will be used automatically without needing to specify this prop.

isItemEqualToValue.name: isItemEqualToValue
isItemEqualToValue.description:
Custom comparison logic used to determine if a combobox item value matches the current selected value. Useful when item values are objects without matching referentially.
Defaults to `Object.is` comparison.

defaultValue.name: defaultValue
defaultValue.description:
The uncontrolled selected value of the combobox when it's initially rendered.

To render a controlled combobox, use the `value` prop instead.

actionsRef.name: actionsRef
actionsRef.description:
A ref to imperative actions.
- `unmount`: When specified, the combobox will not be unmounted when closed.
Instead, the `unmount` function must be called to unmount the combobox manually.
Useful when the combobox's animation is controlled by an external library.

onOpenChange.name: onOpenChange
onOpenChange.description: Event handler called when the popup is opened or closed.

onInputValueChange.name: onInputValueChange
onInputValueChange.description: Event handler called when the input value changes.

onItemHighlighted.name: onItemHighlighted
onItemHighlighted.description:
Callback fired when an item is highlighted or unhighlighted.
Receives the highlighted item value (or `undefined` if no item is highlighted) and event details with a `reason` property describing why the highlight changed.
The `reason` can be:
- `'keyboard'`: the highlight changed due to keyboard navigation.
- `'pointer'`: the highlight changed due to pointer hovering.
- `'none'`: the highlight changed programmatically.
onItemHighlighted.type: ((highlightedValue: Value | undefined, eventDetails: HighlightEventDetails) => void) | undefined

value.name: value
value.description: The selected value of the combobox. Use when controlled.

onValueChange.name: onValueChange
onValueChange.description: Event handler called when the selected value of the combobox changes.
onValueChange.type: ((value: ComboboxValueType<Value, Multiple> | (Multiple extends true ? never : null), eventDetails: ChangeEventDetails) => void) | undefined

useEventCalendarData.description: Transforms raw events into calendar grid data

getEventsForDay.description: Gets events for a specific day

getCategoryById.description: Gets the category object by ID

getCalendarColorClasses.description:
Resolve a category to its full set of Tailwind colour classes. Prefers an
explicit `colorToken`; falls back to legacy `color` parsing.

getCalendarDotClass.description: Convenience: just the dot class (most common lookup).

EventCategory.description:
Event category configuration. Provide a canonical `colorToken` when
possible — it's used everywhere the calendar paints a dot. The free-form
`color`/`bgColor` strings are kept for legacy consumers (legend swatches,
external Tailwind classes) but new code should rely on `colorToken`.

CalendarColorToken.description:
Canonical category palette tokens. Map to a small fixed set of colours so
the calendar can render dots/strokes without parsing arbitrary class names.
Add new tokens here when the design palette grows.

CalendarActionItem.description:
Calendar action item.

The library never navigates on its own — there is no `href` shortcut.
The consumer wires routing into `onClick` (e.g. a Tanstack Router
`navigate(...)` or Inertia `router.visit(...)`), or supplies a fully
pre-rendered element via `element`.

EventCalendarStrings.description: Localizable strings — pass partial overrides via `strings`.

resolveCategoryColorToken.description:
Best-effort mapping from a free-form `category.color` Tailwind class to a
canonical token. Falls back to `gray`. Prefer `category.colorToken` —
this is only used to retrofit legacy consumers.

Storage key for persisting table state (column visibility, etc.) to localStorage.
Pass a simple module name and DataTable handles the internal formatting.

Internally formatted as: `dt.{storageKey}.columns`

When enabled, the DataTable will:
- Load initial column visibility from localStorage
- Automatically save changes to localStorage
- Handle SSR safely (no errors on server)
- Gracefully handle quota exceeded errors

  enableColumnVisibility={true}
/>

LazyImage.description:
LazyImage — a layout-stable image component.

Renders a container of fixed dimensions (driven by `containerClassName`)
with a Skeleton placeholder. Once the browser finishes loading the image,
the skeleton is removed and the image becomes visible.

When the image is already in the browser cache (e.g. table re-render,
dialog close animation), it renders fully visible immediately — no
skeleton, no opacity transition, no flash.

fallback.name: fallback
fallback.description: Fallback rendered on error (no src, or load failure).

containerClassName.name: containerClassName
containerClassName.description: Class applied to the outer container that reserves layout space.

skeletonClassName.name: skeletonClassName
skeletonClassName.description: Class applied to the skeleton placeholder.

variant.name: variant

secondaryEmphasis.name: secondaryEmphasis
secondaryEmphasis.description:
Tunes the prominence of the secondary value when it renders.
`discrete` (default for stacked) — `xxs` discrete tone, easy to scan past.
`muted` — `xs` secondary tone (legacy behaviour).
`match` — same size as primary, regular weight.
`hidden` — never render the secondary even if a pair is provided.

locale.name: locale
locale.description: When set, formats `value` via `Intl.NumberFormat`; ignored if `value` is already a string.

MetadataListStrings.description:
Default user-facing strings for `<MetadataList>`.

Consumers wire backend i18n at the call site:

  <MetadataList strings={{ infoFallback: t('metadata.info') }} … />

solid.name: solid
solid.description: When true, fills the badge with the tone color and inverts the icon.

bordered.name: bordered
bordered.description:
When true, renders a hairline `border-border/60` around the badge.
Useful for compact "secret list" / "key list" rows where the icon
needs a subtle outline against the surrounding card surface
(see `composed/admin/api-key-list`).

title.name: title
title.description:
Headline — typically the resource name in negative form
("No products", "No invoices yet"). Falls back to `strings.title`.

description.name: description
description.description:
Supporting description. Keep it to 1–2 sentences. Falls back to
`strings.description`. Pass `false` to hide entirely (when the title
alone tells the story).

media.name: media
media.description:
The visual: an illustration (use `partials/illustrations/*` or your
own SVG), a Lucide icon, or any ReactNode.

mediaVariant.name: mediaVariant
mediaVariant.description:
Controls the chrome around `media`. Defaults to `'none'`. See
`EmptyStateMediaVariant`.

renderMedia.name: renderMedia
renderMedia.description:
Render-prop alternative to `media` — receives a context so consumers
can drive a fully custom illustration based on the variant.

actions.name: actions
actions.description:
Primary actions (typically a `<Button>`) shown directly under the
description. Pass an array to space them with `gap-2`.

footer.name: footer
footer.description:
Secondary content under the actions — small text, learn-more link,
keyboard hints, etc.

border.name: border
border.description:
`border` toggles the dashed-border look on/off. Defaults to `false`
(no border) — reach for `true` when the empty state replaces a
card body and you want the dashed-zone affordance.

strings.name: strings
strings.description: All copy is overridable via the strings prop, deep-merged.

ariaLabel.name: ariaLabel
ariaLabel.description:
Optional aria-label on the wrapper. Defaults to `strings.ariaLabel`
which itself defaults to "Empty state".

DocumentStackIllustration.description:
Two staggered document silhouettes — for "no documents", "no
invoices", "no reports" surfaces.

InboxCleanIllustration.description:
Open inbox tray with a soft check above — for "all caught up",
"no notifications", "inbox zero" surfaces.

SearchGlassIllustration.description:
Magnifying-glass illustration centered on a soft tinted disk — for
"no search results", "nothing matches your filters" surfaces.

StackedCardsIllustration.description:
Three stacked card silhouettes with a fade-out at the bottom — the
generic "no records" illustration. Token-driven so it adapts to any
theme (uses `bg-muted`, `bg-background`, `border-border`, no raw
palette).

UsersCircleIllustration.description:
Three overlapping avatar circles — for "no team members", "no
customers", "no contributors" surfaces.

defaultExpanded.name: defaultExpanded
defaultExpanded.description:
Initial expanded step ids (uncontrolled). Defaults to opening the
first non-completed step so the user lands on the next thing to do.

multiple.name: multiple
multiple.description: Allow multiple steps expanded simultaneously. Defaults to `false`.

onStepOpen.name: onStepOpen
onStepOpen.description:
Emit when a step is opened (for analytics — fires on transitions
from "not in expanded set" to "in expanded set").

variant.name: variant
variant.description: Visual chrome. Defaults to `'bordered'` (the canonical onboarding shape).

columns.name: columns
columns.description: Optional custom columns. Defaults to Description / Qty / Price / Total.

footerRows.name: footerRows
footerRows.description:
Optional custom footer rows. When omitted and items match the default
`InvoiceLineItem` shape, the legacy auto-computed subtotal / tax / total
footer is used.

taxRate.name: taxRate
taxRate.description: Legacy: tax rate for auto-computed footer (only used with default columns).

currency.name: currency
currency.description: Legacy: currency symbol for auto-computed footer.

scale.name: scale
scale.type: { label: string; value: number; max?: number | undefined; } | undefined

dateTone.name: dateTone
dateTone.description: Tone of the date block. Default `primary`.

timePlacement.name: timePlacement
timePlacement.description: Where to render the booking time. Default `below`.

gradient.name: gradient
gradient.description: Optional gradient (`from-... via-... to-...` Tailwind tokens) for `gradient` variant.

ContactCard.description:
ContactCard — Avatar + name + role + contact details (email, phone, location)
with optional Contact / Profile actions and a status badge.

Contact details render through `<ItemGroup>` + `<Item>` so density and
media slots stay aligned with the rest of the library; the header and CTA
row remain bespoke because they are not Item-shaped.

variant.name: variant
variant.description: Cell variant — restricted to grid-friendly layouts.

columns.name: columns
columns.description: Force a fixed column count. `auto` picks based on cell count.

strings.name: strings
strings.description: Override day labels, month names, and legend copy for i18n.

density.name: density
density.description: Compact = single row with collapsible children; expanded = always show.

sources.name: sources
sources.description: Source list. Order is significant — index drives the displayed number.

variant.name: variant
variant.description: `list` is a vertical row layout; `avatars` is a stacked-favicons strip.

maxAvatars.name: maxAvatars
maxAvatars.description: Number of sources rendered in the avatar strip before the "+N" overflow.

defaultExpanded.name: defaultExpanded
defaultExpanded.description: Render the list expanded by default (only applies to `list` variant).

onSelect.name: onSelect
onSelect.description: Click handler — preferred over `url` for SPA routing.

children.name: children
children.description: Children rendered with the shimmer gradient. Defaults to a placeholder string.

paused.name: paused
paused.description: Disable animation (renders the text in muted-foreground).

streaming.name: streaming
streaming.description: Show the live "Thinking…" header with shimmer.

durationSeconds.name: durationSeconds
durationSeconds.description: Total elapsed time in seconds — shown in the collapsed-done header.

description.name: description
description.description: Short description from the package manifest.

publishedAt.name: publishedAt
publishedAt.description: ISO date or human label of last publish.

weeklyDownloads.name: weeklyDownloads
weeklyDownloads.description: Weekly downloads — formatted by the consumer.

keywords.name: keywords
keywords.description: Comma-separated tag list rendered as small chips.

onSelect.name: onSelect
onSelect.description: Click handler for the whole card.

plainText.name: plainText
plainText.description: Plain-text version of the message content; used for the copy action.

index.name: index
index.description: 1-indexed citation number rendered inside the pill.

title.name: title
title.description: Title of the cited source — used in the tooltip / aria-label.

url.name: url
url.description: Optional URL — when provided, the pill renders as a link.

onSelect.name: onSelect
onSelect.description: Click handler — preferred over `url` for SPA / virtual link routing.

nodes.name: nodes
nodes.description: Root nodes — typically one entry but the API accepts multiple.

selectedId.name: selectedId
selectedId.description: Selected node id — drives the active row tint.

onSelect.name: onSelect
onSelect.description: Click handler — fires for files only by default.

hideStatusLabels.name: hideStatusLabels
hideStatusLabels.description: Hide the diff status pills (still tints the row).

status.name: status
status.description: Current status — when set, the action row is replaced with a result pill.

code.name: code
code.description: The code to render. Pre-formatted text.

filename.name: filename
filename.description: Optional filename rendered in the header next to the language label.

headerActions.name: headerActions
headerActions.description: Trailing slot inside the header (e.g. extra action button).

onCopy.name: onCopy
onCopy.description: Fired when the user copies (after the navigator.clipboard write).

streaming.name: streaming
streaming.description: Show the streaming hint under the header.

meta.name: meta
meta.description: Optional secondary label — formatted size, file extension, etc.

kind.name: kind
kind.description: Pre-categorized kind. Defaults to "generic" — drives the fallback icon.

thumbnailUrl.name: thumbnailUrl
thumbnailUrl.description: When set, renders the chip as a thumbnail tile with this image.

progress.name: progress
progress.description: Show a small loading bar (in-flight upload).

onOpen.name: onOpen
onOpen.description: Open / download callback (clicking the chip body).

subtitle.name: subtitle
subtitle.description: Subtitle — typically the kind / language ("React component", "Markdown").

icon.name: icon
icon.description: Icon override for the leading badge.

copyText.name: copyText
copyText.description: Plain text content used by the default copy action.

children.name: children
children.description: Body content — preview, code, sandbox iframe, etc.

collapsed.name: collapsed
collapsed.description: Hide the body and render the artifact as a clickable preview-only chip.

onOpen.name: onOpen
onOpen.description: Click handler for the whole header (e.g. open in side panel).

onDownload.name: onDownload
onDownload.description: Provide a download handler — adds a download button.

icon.name: icon
icon.description: Avatar / mark — typically an icon or short emoji.

avatar.name: avatar
avatar.description: Override avatar with arbitrary content (image, gradient mark, etc.).

subtitle.name: subtitle
subtitle.description: Short blurb / model identifier rendered under the name.

tone.name: tone
tone.description: Tone color for the avatar wash.

status.name: status
status.description: Live status displayed as a pill next to the name.

variant.name: variant
variant.description: Density variant — `inline` is a single-line chip, `card` is a tile.

defaultOpen.name: defaultOpen
defaultOpen.description:
Whether the section is initially expanded. Uncontrolled — the
collapsible state is owned by the component.

onOpenChange.name: onOpenChange
onOpenChange.description: Open-state change callback for controlled mode.

onAdd.name: onAdd
onAdd.description: Add-new-key click handler. When omitted the add button is hidden.

onDelete.name: onDelete
onDelete.description: Per-item delete handler. When omitted the Delete menu item is hidden.

onCopy.name: onCopy
onCopy.description:
Per-item copy callback fired AFTER the value has been written to the
clipboard — useful for analytics. The library handles the actual
write via `base/copyable`.

onError.name: onError
onError.description: Fires on fetcher rejection — consumer wires telemetry / toasts here.

footerSlot.name: footerSlot

maxLength.name: maxLength

ActiveFilterItem.description:
Active Filter Item Component

This component displays a single active filter in the filter list.
It provides:
- Display of filter label and value
- Remove filter functionality
- Visual feedback for active state
- Support for different filter types

The component is used in the filter list to show currently active filters
and allow users to remove them.

AsyncFilterContent.description:
Async Filter Content – renders inside the popover pipeline.

Matches the layout of RegularFilterContent (header + command list + footer)
but fetches options asynchronously via TanStack Query.

FilterListContent.description:
Filter Popover Content Component

This component provides the content wrapper for filter popovers.
It handles:
- Consistent popover styling
- Content layout and spacing
- Responsive behavior
- Animation and transitions

The component is used to wrap filter content in a consistent
popover interface across different filter types.

FilterListItem.description:
FilterListItem — single row inside the Filters-button menu.

Shows a leading icon (rendered as-is so its native colour comes through),
the filter label as the primary line, and a secondary "{n} options" line
for SELECT-style filters or the "select date range" hint for DATE filters.

All typography flows through the base Text component so the global
scale + theming applies consistently.

FilterOperatorSelect.description:
FilterOperatorSelect — operator dropdown for an active filter pill.

Now delegates the trigger + popover + command-list pattern entirely to
`PopoverMenu`. The trigger is rendered in the pill's inline strip, the
`header` slot supplies the small "Operator" eyebrow, and item rendering
stays compact (xs typography, density-tokenized padding from base/command).

segmentResetClass.name: segmentResetClass
segmentResetClass.description:
Segment-reset classes forwarded by the parent pill so the trigger
drops its base border + focus ring (it lives inside a segmented
pill, not as a standalone button).

availableFilters.name: availableFilters
availableFilters.description: Filters to render in the "+" list inside the popover.

activeFilters.name: activeFilters
activeFilters.description: Active regular filters list (for the popover header / context).

operatorChangeRef.name: operatorChangeRef
operatorChangeRef.description: External signal to suppress popover open during operator change.

FilterPopoverContent.description:
Filter Popover Content Component

This component provides the content wrapper for filter popovers.
It handles:
- Consistent popover styling
- Content layout and spacing
- Responsive behavior
- Animation and transitions

The component is used to wrap filter content in a consistent
popover interface across different filter types.

FilterTabs.description:
Horizontal tab row for filter presets (Shopify admin style).

- "All" tab (empty presets): active when zero non-search active filters
- Named tabs: active when presets exactly match non-search active filters
- Search filters are preserved across tab switches

FilterValueDisplay.description:
Filter Value Display Component

This component provides the UI for displaying filter values.
It handles:
- Displaying filter values with appropriate formatting
- Showing icons for filter options
- Handling date formatting
- Supporting internationalization

The component is used to show users the current value of a filter
in a consistent and informative way.

FiltersButton.description:
Filters Button Component

This component provides the main button for adding and managing filters.
It handles:
- Opening the filter popover
- Managing filter selection state
- Handling filter actions (select, back, close)
- Supporting internationalization

The component is used as the primary entry point for users to
add and configure filters in the application.

RegularFilterContent.description:
RegularFilterContent — popover body for SELECT / MULTI_SELECT filters.

Layout:
  - Compact header: optional back-arrow, label as small uppercase eyebrow.
  - Inline search input (only if the filter has more than 5 options).
  - Tight list of options (sm padding, xs font, ml-auto check on the right).
  - Compact footer: selected count on the leading edge, Confirm button on
    the trailing edge — only rendered when the user has changed selection.

Sizes are tuned to feel like a native menu, not a card. Popover width
stays at ~260-300px so labels read at one glance.

SearchFiltersList.description:
Search Filters List Component

This component provides a list of search filters that are always visible.
It handles:
- Rendering multiple search filters
- Managing filter values
- Applying custom styling
- Supporting responsive layout

The component is used to display search filters that should be
immediately accessible to users without requiring a popover.

TagsFilterContent.description:
Tags Filter Content Component

This component provides the content for tags filter popovers.
It handles:
- Tag input and management
- Multiple tag selection
- Displaying and removing tags
- Applying filter changes

The component wraps the TagsFacet component and provides
the popover-specific UI elements like header and action buttons.

AsyncSelectFacet.description:
Async Select Filter Facet

A TanStack Query-powered filter facet for server-side option loading.
Supports:
- Live typeahead search with debounce + AbortSignal
- Loading, error, empty, min-query-hint states
- Preload (fetch default options on open)
- Multi-select with maxSelected enforcement
- Options label cache for pill display after popover close

onChange.type: (value: { start?: Date | undefined; end?: Date | undefined; }) => void

RangeFacet.description:
Range Filter Facet

This component implements a numeric range filter that allows users to:
- Set minimum and maximum values
- Configure step size and precision
- Set value constraints (min/max allowed values)
- Customize the range input appearance

The component integrates with the filter context to manage the range values
and provides a user-friendly interface for range selection.

value.name: value

onChange.name: onChange
onChange.type: (value: { min?: number | undefined; max?: number | undefined; }) => void

SelectFacet.description:
SelectFacet — single/multi-select dropdown filter.

Composes `PopoverMenu` for the popover + searchable list pattern.
Multi-select keeps the popover open after a tap; single-select closes
unless `closeOnSelect=false` is passed in the filter config.
